title: Memory, OCR, and UI Detection Flow
interaction_flow: Caller → procmem_scanner: scan_memory(pid, pattern, context_size=256, mode='auto', llm_mode=True)
procmem_scanner → YARA: build_rules(pattern, mode) — compiles hex or string YARA rule → rules
procmem_scanner → kernel32: ReadProcessMemory(h_proc, BaseAddress, buf, RegionSize) → raw bytes
procmem_scanner → YARA rules: rules.match(data=raw_bytes) → matches (offset, matched_data)
procmem_scanner → procmem_scanner: format_llm_context(data, offset, base_addr, length=64) → {address, offset, hex, ascii, hit_pos}
procmem_scanner → Caller: return list[dict{address, offset, hex, ascii, hit_pos}]
ocr_utils → ocr_utils: _get_rapid() — lazy init RapidOCR singleton
ocr_utils → Caller: return {text: str, lines: [str], details: [{bbox, text, conf:float}]}
ocr_utils → user32.PrintWindow: PrintWindow(hwnd, hdc, 3) → bitmap bits
ocr_utils → ocr_utils: ocr_image(img) → (same as Screen OCR path above)
ui_detect → YOLO Model: YOLO(model_path) — load OmniParser icon_detect weights
ui_detect → YOLO Model: model(image_path, conf=threshold, verbose=False) → results
ui_detect → ui_detect: extract boxes → [{bbox:[x1,y1,x2,y2], confidence, class}]
Caller → ui_detect: visualize(image_path, detections, ocr_results, output_path)
ui_detect → ui_detect: draw red rects (UI boxes) + blue polygons (OCR text) → save PNG + JSON
adb_ui → ADB shell: _dump_native() [fallback] — adb shell uiautomator dump → adb pull ui.xml
adb_ui → Caller: print nodes OR return raw node list
ljqCtrl → PIL.ImageGrab: ImageGrab.grab(wrect) → screen PIL Image
ljqCtrl → cv2: matchTemplate(screen_bgr, template_bgr, TM_CCOEFF_NORMED) → max_val, max_loc
Caller → ljqCtrl: Click(x, y)  [physical coords from screenshot]
ljqCtrl → win32api: keybd_event(VK_CODE[key], 0, 0, 0) for each key in cmd.split('+')
ljqCtrl → win32api: keybd_event(VK_CODE[key], 0, KEYEVENTF_KEYUP, 0) in reverse
Caller → ljqCtrl: GrabWindow(hwnd) → win32gui.SetForegroundWindow + ImageGrab.grab → PIL Image
key_relationships: procmem_scanner depends_on kernel32 via ctypes (OpenProcess, VirtualQueryEx, ReadProcessMemory, CloseHandle)
procmem_scanner produces format_llm_context dicts → consumed by LLM caller
ocr_utils depends_on PIL.ImageGrab (screen capture) and win32gui/user32.PrintWindow (window capture)
ocr_window uses PrintWindow (RDP-safe) then delegates_to ocr_image
ui_detect main() orchestrates detect_ui_elements + ocr_text + visualize
adb_ui depends_on uiautomator2 (u2, preferred) with native adb fallback
adb_ui._parse_xml uses xml.etree.ElementTree + re for bounds parsing
adb_ui.tap issues adb shell input tap via subprocess
ljqCtrl depends_on cv2.matchTemplate for template-based element finding
ljqCtrl.Click → SetCursorPos (physical DPI scaling) → MouseClick → win32api mouse_event
ljqCtrl.Press → VK_CODE lookup → win32api.keybd_event (down + up for each key)

data_structures.format_llm_context output: {address: hex_str, offset: hex_str, hex: str, ascii: str, hit_pos: int}
data_structures.ocr result: {text: str, lines: [str], details: [{bbox: [[x,y]*4], text: str, conf: float}]}
data_structures.adb_ui node: {text: str, click: bool, edit: bool, cx: int, cy: int, cls: str, rid: str}
data_structures.MEMORY_BASIC_INFORMATION: {BaseAddress, AllocationBase, AllocationProtect, RegionSize, State, Protect, Type}

important_notes: procmem_scanner: Windows-only (ctypes.windll.kernel32), 64-bit user space (max_addr=0x7FFFFFFFFFFF), YARA used as pattern engine for both hex and ASCII/wide string patterns
ocr_utils: _rapid_engine is a module-level singleton; conf from RapidOCR is str, cast to float explicitly; ImageGrab fails on disconnected RDP — use ocr_window(hwnd) instead
ui_detect: model loaded fresh each call (no caching); DEFAULT_MODEL path = ../temp/weights/icon_detect/model.pt relative to file; OCR is optional (HAS_OCR guard)
adb_ui: u2 preferred (no idle requirement, better for animated apps); filters out 'termux' package nodes; bounds parsed via regex r'\[(\d+),(\d+)\]'

ljqCtrl: CRITICAL — do NOT import pyautogui (conflicts with win32api); dpi_scale computed at import time from DESKTOPHORZRES vs SM_CXSCREEN; Click/SetCursorPos expect physical coordinates; Press parses 'ctrl+v' style strings via VK_CODE dict
